common.quota_update_required: Please update the CPA version or check for updates
common.custom_headers_hint: Optional HTTP headers to send with the request. Leave blank to remove.
common.unsaved_changes_message: You have unsaved changes. Leaving now will discard them. Do you want to leave?

auto_login.title: Auto Login in Progress...
auto_login.message: Attempting to connect to server using locally saved connection information

login.subtitle: Please enter connection information to access the management interface
login.connection_auto_hint: The system will automatically use the current URL for connection
login.custom_connection_hint: By default the current URL is used. Override it here if needed.
login.cpa_connection_hint: Manager Server uses this URL to reach the CPA Management API and RESP usage queue.
login.usage_service_mode_hint: This panel is hosted by Manager Server. Enter the CPA API URL.
login.usage_service_configured_hint: This panel is hosted by a configured Manager Server. After login it uses the saved CPA connection on the server.
login.docker_setup_subtitle: Initialize this Docker deployment with the admin key from the first startup log.
login.docker_login_subtitle: Use the Manager Server admin key to manage the whole panel.
login.admin_key_placeholder: Enter the admin key from the startup log
login.admin_key_hint: This key authenticates the Manager Server panel. It is different from the CPA Management Key.
login.admin_key_setup_hint: The admin key was printed once in the Manager Server startup log.
login.migration_detected_hint: Historical data was detected. The server will keep it and migrate legacy settings automatically.
login.cpa_management_key_hint: Only used by Manager Server to validate and manage the CPA upstream.
login.request_monitoring_enabled_hint: Saving will automatically enable CPA usage statistics and start the Manager Server collector.
login.request_monitoring_disabled_hint: The CPA connection will still be saved, but CPA usage statistics and the collector will not be enabled. If CPA usage statistics were enabled elsewhere, re-enabling the collector within the queue retention window may still collect retained events.
login.poll_interval_hint: Must be less than or equal to the CPA usage queue retention window. Saving will automatically enable CPA usage statistics.
login.error_required: Please fill in complete connection information
login.error_invalid: Connection failed, please check address and key
login.error_network: Network connection failed, please check your network or server address
login.error_timeout: Connection timed out, server not responding
login.error_not_found: Server address invalid or management API not enabled
login.error_server: Internal server error, please try again later
login.error_cors: Cross-origin request blocked, please check server configuration

account_actions.title: Auth Issue Handling
account_actions.description: When request monitoring sees a revoked OAuth token, unavailable authentication, or a deactivated workspace, CPA Manager Plus records an auth issue candidate here for you to confirm deletion, re-enable, ignore, or mark resolved.
account_actions.empty_desc: Auth issues will appear here when request monitoring captures token_revoked, invalidated oauth token, or similar authentication failures.
account_actions.confirm_delete_message: This will delete auth file "{{file}}". This action cannot be undone. Confirm only when you are sure the account cannot recover.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Welcome to CPA Manager Plus
dashboard.caring_morning: A fresh start — let's make today count.
dashboard.caring_night: Burning the midnight oil? Don't forget to rest.
dashboard.traffic_overview_subtitle: Calls, tokens, and hourly load for today
dashboard.request_health_timeline_desc: Today's request health, aggregated every 10 minutes.

auth_files.title: Auth Files Management
auth_files.description: Manage all CLI Proxy JSON auth files here (e.g. Qwen, Gemini, Vertex). Uploading a credential immediately enables the corresponding AI integration.
auth_files.paste_cpa_hint: The JSON will be stored as-is after validating it is a JSON object.
auth_files.paste_session_hint: ChatGPT Web session JSON is converted to a CPA Codex auth file before upload. Tokens are sensitive credentials.
auth_files.paste_sub2api_hint: Official sub2api account export JSON is converted by extracting OpenAI OAuth accounts into CPA Codex auth files. Tokens are sensitive credentials.
auth_files.paste_error_file_name_invalid: Use a .json file name without path separators or reserved characters
auth_files.paste_error_save_in_progress: Auth JSON paste save is already in progress
auth_files.empty_desc: Click the button above to upload the first file
auth_files.search_empty_desc: Try changing the filters or clearing the search box.
auth_files.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete file
auth_files.delete_all_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete all auth files? This operation cannot be undone!
auth_files.delete_filtered_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete all {{type}} auth files? This operation cannot be undone!
auth_files.delete_problem_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete all problematic auth files? This operation cannot be undone!
auth_files.delete_problem_filtered_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete all problematic {{type}} auth files? This operation cannot be undone!
auth_files.delete_filtered_result_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete auth files in the current filtered results? This operation cannot be undone!
auth_files.delete_filtered_result_confirm_file_scop: Delete the auth files behind the current filtered results? Deletion is by file name; if multiple account rows come from the same auth file, the whole file will be removed. This cannot be undone!
auth_files.delete_filtered_partial: {{type}} auth files deletion finished: {{success}} succeeded, {{failed}} failed
auth_files.delete_filtered_none: No deletable auth files under the current filter ({{type}})
auth_files.delete_problem_partial: Problematic auth files deletion finished: {{success}} succeeded, {{failed}} failed
auth_files.delete_problem_filtered_partial: Problematic {{type}} auth files deletion finished: {{success}} succeeded, {{failed}} failed
auth_files.delete_problem_none: No deletable problematic auth files under the current filter
auth_files.delete_problem_filtered_none: No deletable problematic {{type}} auth files under the current filter
auth_files.delete_filtered_result_success: Deleted {{count}} auth files from the filtered results successfully
auth_files.delete_filtered_result_partial: Filtered result deletion finished: {{success}} succeeded, {{failed}} failed
auth_files.delete_filtered_result_none: No deletable auth files in the current filtered results
auth_files.search_placeholder: Search name, type, auth index, account ID, plan, or error status. Use * as a wildcard
auth_files.too_many_files_warning: Too many credentials. Showing all may cause performance issues, please use paged view.
auth_files.models_empty: No available models for this credential
auth_files.models_empty_desc: This credential may not be loaded by the server yet, or no models are bound to it.
auth_files.models_unsupported: This feature is not supported in the current version
auth_files.models_unsupported_desc: Please update CLI Proxy API to the latest version and try again
auth_files.codex_status_badge_http_401_title: The latest Codex check returned 401, which usually requires OAuth reauthorization.
auth_files.codex_status_badge_reauth_title: The latest Codex check returned 401 or suggested reauthorization. Reauthorize with OAuth before using it again.
auth_files.codex_status_badge_five_hour_limited_tit: The Codex 5-hour quota window is at its limit and usually only needs the short window to recover.
auth_files.codex_status_badge_weekly_limited_title: The Codex 7-day quota window is at its limit and usually needs time to recover.
auth_files.codex_status_badge_monthly_limited_title: The Codex monthly quota window is at its limit and usually needs time to recover.
auth_files.codex_status_badge_disabled_reset_title: This disabled Codex account has a known quota recovery time: {{reset}}.
auth_files.batch_delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{count}} files?
auth_files.batch_fields_partial: Account field update finished: {{success}} succeeded, {{failed}} failed
auth_files.priority_hint: Integers only. Invalid values are ignored. Larger value means higher priority.
auth_files.excluded_models_placeholder: Comma or newline separated, e.g. model-a, gpt-5-*, *-preview
auth_files.excluded_models_hint: Saved as an array and normalized by trim/lowercase/dedup/sort.
auth_files.disable_cooling_hint: Supports booleans, numeric 0/non-0, and strings like true/false/1/0; unparseable values are ignored.
auth_files.note_placeholder: Enter a note, e.g.: John's account
auth_files.note_hint: Optional. Used to describe the purpose or owner of this credential; leave empty to omit.
auth_files.headers_hint: Enter custom HTTP headers as a JSON object, e.g., {"X-My-Header": "value"}
auth_files.headers_invalid_json: Custom headers must be valid JSON.
auth_files.headers_invalid_object: Custom headers must be a JSON object.
auth_files.headers_invalid_value: Each custom header value must be a string.
auth_files.prefix_proxy_invalid_json: This auth file is not a JSON object, so fields cannot be edited.
auth_files.prefix_proxy_html_challenge: Downloaded content is an HTML challenge page, not an auth JSON object. Re-authenticate or replace this auth file before editing fields.
auth_files.quota_cooldown_badge_title: This auth file is in a CPAMP-managed quota cooldown and will be recovered automatically. It is not the native CPA disabled state. Owner: {{owner}}. Expected recovery: {{recoverAt}}.

vertex_import.title: Vertex JSON Login
vertex_import.description: Upload a Google service account JSON to store it as auth-dir/vertex-<project>.json using the same rules as the CLI vertex-import helper.
vertex_import.location_hint: Leave empty to use the default region us-central1.
vertex_import.file_hint: Only Google Cloud service account key JSON files are accepted.

oauth_excluded.title: OAuth Model Disablement
oauth_excluded.description: Per-provider model disablement is shown as cards; click a card to edit or delete. Wildcards * are supported and the scope follows the auth file filter.
oauth_excluded.provider_hint: Defaults to the current filter; pick an existing provider or type a new name.
oauth_excluded.models_unsupported: Current CPA version does not support fetching model lists.
oauth_excluded.models_loaded: {{count}} models loaded. Check the models to disable.
oauth_excluded.no_models_available: No models available for this provider.
oauth_excluded.list_empty_all: No provider model disablement yet; click “Add Disablement” to create one.
oauth_excluded.list_empty_filtered: No disabled items in this scope; click “Add Disablement” to add.
oauth_excluded.disconnected: Connect to the server to view model disablement
oauth_excluded.upgrade_required: Current CPA version does not support OAuth model disablement. Please upgrade.
oauth_excluded.upgrade_required_desc: The current server version does not support fetching OAuth model disablement. Please upgrade to the latest CPA (CLI Proxy API) version and try again.

oauth_model_alias.title: OAuth Model Aliases
oauth_model_alias.provider_hint: Defaults to the current filter; pick an existing provider or type a new name.
oauth_model_alias.model_source_unsupported: The current CPA version does not support fetching model lists (manual input still works).
oauth_model_alias.model_source_loaded: {{count}} models loaded. Use the dropdown in 'Source model name', or type custom values. Saving an empty list removes that provider. Enable 'Keep original' to keep the original name while adding the alias.
oauth_model_alias.delete_alias_confirm: Delete alias {{alias}} and unmap all associated models?
oauth_model_alias.list_empty_all: No model aliases yet—use “Add Alias” to create one.
oauth_model_alias.diagram_settings_empty: No mappings for this alias yet.
oauth_model_alias.diagram_tap_hint: On touch devices: tap a source model, then tap an alias to link.
oauth_model_alias.upgrade_required: This feature requires a newer CLI Proxy API (CPA) version. Please upgrade.
oauth_model_alias.upgrade_required_desc: The current server does not support the OAuth model aliases API. Please upgrade to the latest CLI Proxy API (CPA) version.

auth_login.codex_oauth_hint: Login to Codex service through OAuth flow, automatically obtain and save authentication files.
auth_login.anthropic_oauth_hint: Login to Anthropic (Claude) service through OAuth flow, automatically obtain and save authentication files.
auth_login.antigravity_oauth_hint: Login to Antigravity service (Google account) through OAuth flow, automatically obtain and save authentication files.
auth_login.kimi_oauth_hint: Login to Kimi service through OAuth device flow, automatically obtain and save authentication files.
auth_login.xai_oauth_hint: Login to xAI Grok through OAuth flow, automatically obtain and save authentication files.
auth_login.plugin_oauth_hint: Login through the {{plugin}} plugin OAuth flow, then wait for the plugin to save the authentication file.
auth_login.xai_callback_placeholder: Paste the code shown by Grok, or the full callback URL
auth_login.xai_callback_hint: Grok may only show a code on the page. Paste that code directly and the app will submit it as http://127.0.0.1:56121/callback?... automatically.
auth_login.xai_callback_required: Please paste the code shown by Grok or the full callback URL first.
auth_login.xai_callback_state_missing: Missing the state for this xAI login. Start xAI login again, then submit the code.
auth_login.oauth_callback_hint: Remote browser mode: after the provider redirects to http://localhost:..., copy the full URL and submit it here.
auth_login.oauth_callback_required: Please paste the full redirect URL first.
auth_login.oauth_callback_success: Callback URL submitted. Continue waiting for authentication.
auth_login.oauth_callback_upgrade_hint: Please update CLI Proxy API or check the connection.
auth_login.oauth_callback_status_success: Callback URL submitted, waiting for authentication...
auth_login.missing_state: Unable to retrieve authentication state parameter
auth_login.iflow_oauth_hint: Login to iFlow service through OAuth flow, automatically obtain and save authentication files.
auth_login.remote_access_disabled: This login method is not available for remote access. Please access from localhost.

model_prices.title: Model Price Management
model_prices.subtitle: Maintain model pricing in Manager Server, sync high-confidence prices automatically, and confirm similar candidates manually.
model_prices.sync_idle: Sync currently used models from LiteLLM, OpenRouter, and other pricing metadata sources. Ambiguous matches will be listed for confirmation.
model_prices.manual_hint: Saved prices use the local model name as the key; manual saves are marked manual, while confirmed candidates keep their actual source and source model ID.

usage_stats.title: Usage Statistics
usage_stats.import_success: Import complete: added {{added}}, skipped {{skipped}}, total {{total}}, failed {{failed}}
usage_stats.import_file_too_large: Import file must be no larger than {{maxSize}}
usage_stats.import_confirm_body: {{name}} will be written into the Manager Server SQLite usage database. Duplicate events will be skipped automatically.
usage_stats.import_legacy_warning: Legacy export files are missing some request metadata, so account matching and detail accuracy may be lower than newly collected data.
usage_stats.import_export_requires_usage_service: Import and export require Manager Server. Open the Manager Server-hosted panel to use this feature.
usage_stats.chart_line_hint: Show up to 9 model lines at once
usage_stats.total_cost_hint: Token-based estimate using configured model pricing
usage_stats.model_price_sync_requires_usage_service: Price sync requires Manager Server. Open the Manager Server-hosted panel to use this feature.
usage_stats.model_price_model_required: Please choose a model to set pricing
usage_stats.cost_need_price: Set a model price to view cost stats
usage_stats.cost_need_usage: No usage data available to calculate cost
usage_stats.latency_unit_hint: Durations use backend field {{field}} and are interpreted as {{unit}} before formatting.
usage_stats.request_events_empty_desc: No request details are available for the selected time range.
usage_stats.request_events_no_result_desc: Try different filters or clear the current filters.
usage_stats.request_events_limit_hint: Showing {{shown}} of {{total}} events to keep rendering responsive.

monitoring.title: Request Monitoring
monitoring.subtitle: A unified operations view across model-level calls, upstream channels, auth credentials, and estimated cost. KPIs default to model calls, while task buckets are shown separately so usage.total_requests is not mistaken for business request count.
monitoring.console_subtitle: Track call volume, success and failure, token structure, and spend by account within the selected time range, with a live table that behaves more like a request log console.
monitoring.custom_range_required: Select both start and end times.
monitoring.custom_range_invalid: End time cannot be earlier than start time.
monitoring.search_placeholder: Search account, label, auth index, model, channel, source, credential, API key, or endpoint path
monitoring.active_filters_hint: {{count}} filters active, {{rows}} calls match the current scope.
monitoring.auth_health_meta: Credential metadata is mapped back to usage details
monitoring.usage_disabled_body: Request Monitoring depends on Manager Server or compatible usage data. Enable usage statistics and verify Manager Server is running to populate runtime metrics here.
monitoring.request_monitoring_disabled_body: CPA Manager Plus is not currently running the request collector. Enable request monitoring in CPA Manager Plus Configuration when you need this page.
monitoring.request_monitoring_service_unavailable_b: Request monitoring is unavailable from this Manager Server. Check setup, collector status, and the bound CPA connection.
monitoring.request_monitoring_not_configured_body: Request monitoring is available only from the Manager Server-hosted panel after setup. CPA panel mode does not support usage-statistics features.
monitoring.empty_diagnostics_body: If CPA has produced requests but this view is still empty, confirm usage publishing is enabled, check Manager Server /status, and make sure only one Manager Server consumes the same CPA usage queue.
monitoring.zero_token_notice_body: There are {{count}} zero-token model calls in the current range ({{models}}). These models are usually not billed purely by tokens, so cost remains a partial estimate.
monitoring.estimated_cost_hint: Only covers models with configured pricing and token-based billing.
monitoring.estimated_cost_missing: Model pricing is not configured yet.
monitoring.zero_token_model_calls_hint: Useful for spotting image or other non-pure-token billing models.
monitoring.token_signal_desc: Separates input, output, reasoning, and cache reuse so spend structure and cache efficiency are visible together.
monitoring.metric_scope_note: Top-level KPIs use model-call semantics inside the selected window. Task metrics are approximate buckets grouped by timestamp + source + credential.
monitoring.requests_trend_desc: Aggregates requests and token volume by {{granularity}} to expose bursts, rhythm changes, and cost pressure.
monitoring.model_mix_desc: Highlights the dominant models in the current filter scope so hotspots are obvious.
monitoring.hourly_requests_desc: Shows where demand concentrates within a day.
monitoring.hourly_tokens_desc: Shows which hours absorb the most token volume for capacity and spend analysis.
monitoring.channel_table_desc: Merges usage, openai-compatibility, and auth-files to inspect channel throughput, success rate, and latency.
monitoring.model_table_desc: Tracks request volume, success rate, token usage, and latency by model.
monitoring.failure_table_desc: Surfaces the noisiest failing sources and channels first, so account or route issues are visible early.
monitoring.realtime_health_desc: Connection status, credential enablement, and recent failure signals update with the current filter window.
monitoring.account_models_desc: Model-level spend and call distribution for {{account}} in the current scope.
monitoring.api_key_summary_desc: Aggregates calls, success/failure, total tokens, and estimated cost by client API key in the current filter scope. Expand a row to inspect token structure and model usage.
monitoring.api_key_summary_description: These keys are caller/client API keys. Aliases are preferred when available; expanded rows show input, output, cached tokens, and per-model calls and spend for that key.
monitoring.account_overview_title_hint: Real-time per-account roll-up of calls, tokens and cost within the current filter scope; expand for model details and quota usage.
monitoring.account_overview_description: Real-time per-account roll-up of calls, success/failure, token structure and total cost within the current filter scope. Expand a row to inspect model details and quota usage.
monitoring.account_overview_health_hint: Based on request statistics in the current filter scope.
monitoring.account_overview_status_hint: Current account enabled state summarized from linked auth files.
monitoring.account_overview_success_rate_hint: Successful calls divided by total calls in the current filter scope.
monitoring.codex_inspection_desc: Inspect Codex accounts independently for quota exhaustion, invalid accounts, and disabled recovery. Delete, disable, and enable actions are executed only after confirmation.
monitoring.codex_inspection_mode_local_desc: Runs through the local flow behind the current browser connection and depends on this connection and browser session. Use it for quick checks on the current device.
monitoring.codex_inspection_mode_server_desc: Submits the inspection to Manager Server. It supports scheduled runs, background execution, and persisted history for batch checks and audit.
monitoring.codex_inspection_logs_desc: Probe and execution logs are streamed in realtime and can be collapsed after the run completes.
monitoring.codex_inspection_results_desc: Summarize account status, invalid-account errors, and the next suggested actions. Supports batch and row-level execution.
monitoring.codex_inspection_sampled_hint: Random sampling is enabled, so only sampled accounts generate suggested actions.
monitoring.codex_inspection_error_summary_reauth: Authentication is invalid and requires re-login
monitoring.codex_inspection_empty: No inspection result yet. Start the inspection first.
monitoring.codex_inspection_execute_confirm_body: {{total}} actions will be executed: delete {{delete}}, disable {{disable}}, enable {{enable}}. Continue?
monitoring.codex_inspection_auto_execute_started: Inspection completed. Automatically executing {{count}} actions with “{{mode}}”.
monitoring.codex_inspection_auto_execute_no_actions: Inspection completed. No issues requiring action were found.
monitoring.codex_inspection_auto_execute_skipped_by: Inspection completed. The current “{{mode}}” strategy has no auto-executable actions, so {{count}} suggestions remain pending for manual handling.
monitoring.codex_inspection_auto_execute_summary_su: Automatic execution completed: processed {{total}} actions, succeeded {{success}}, and no pending actions remain.
monitoring.codex_inspection_auto_execute_summary_pa: Automatic execution completed: processed {{total}} actions, succeeded {{success}}, failed {{failed}}, and {{remaining}} pending actions remain.
monitoring.codex_inspection_delete_reauth_confirm_b: {{count}} auth files for accounts that require re-login will be deleted. Deletion is performed by file name and cannot be undone. Continue?
monitoring.codex_inspection_delete_reauth_single_bo: Delete auth file {{file}} for account {{account}}? This cannot be undone.
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_desc: Configure the target type, concurrency, timeout, and threshold for Codex account inspection. Saved values are used for future runs in this browser.
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_auto_execute_a: Automatically execute suggested actions after inspection
When enabled, delete, disable, and enable actions run automatically after inspection. Once execution finishes, the current workflow ends and inspection will not restart automatically.
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_invalid_intege: {{field}} must be an integer no smaller than {{min}}
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_invalid_thresh: {{field}} must be a number between 0 and 100
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_sample_size_hi: Use 0 to inspect all accounts. Values above 0 only sample probes and do not change the action policy.
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_threshold_hint: Accounts at or above this usage are treated as over threshold. The default 100 only suggests disable when quota is exhausted.
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_auto_execute_w: Auto execution will delete, disable, or enable accounts directly. Only enable it when you trust the current threshold and sampling policy.
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_auto_action_mo: Only record inspection results and suggested actions without changing accounts.
Automatically enable recovered accounts and leave problem accounts for manual handling.
Execute delete or disable suggestions as account disable actions.
Automatically delete invalid accounts and disable over-threshold accounts.
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_auto_execution: Generate inspection results and suggestions only. No account will be enabled, disabled, or deleted.
Automatically enable recovered accounts and handle problem accounts by the strategy below.
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_problem_action: Only auto-enable recovered accounts. Delete and disable suggestions stay manual.
Auto-enable recovered accounts and execute delete or disable suggestions as disable actions.
Auto-enable recovered accounts, delete invalid accounts, and disable over-threshold accounts.
monitoring.codex_inspection_settings_auto_action_mo: Only output results never changes accounts. Auto execute always enables recovered accounts automatically.
Auto execution only enables recovered accounts. Problem accounts remain in the inspection results for manual handling.
Auto execution enables recovered accounts and converts delete or disable suggestions into disable actions. It will not delete accounts automatically.
Auto execution enables recovered accounts, deletes invalid accounts, and disables over-threshold accounts. Use it only when the current threshold and sampling policy are trusted.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_desc: Manager Server inspects Codex accounts on schedule, with persisted results and error logs.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_run_confirm_body: The server will inspect accounts with the current configuration. If automatic handling is enabled, accounts may be disabled or deleted directly. Continue?
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_config_desc: Configure the backend scheduled task trigger, probe concurrency, timeout, and automatic handling strategy.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_config_invalid: Check the inspection config. Time points must be HH:mm and numeric values must be in range.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_close_unsaved_hi: Your configuration hasn't been saved. Closing will discard these changes.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_unsaved_apply_hi: Scheduled tasks use these changes only after save and apply.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_saved_applied: Current configuration is saved and active.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_time_points_hint: Supports multiple HH:mm values separated by commas, spaces, or line breaks.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_interval_inactiv: Currently triggered by time points, so frequency will not take effect.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_time_points_inac: Currently triggered by frequency, so time points will not take effect.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_effect_hint: Takes effect in ≤30 seconds after save.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_server_time_hint: Uses the Manager Server local timezone.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_auto_mode_note_n: Only records inspection results and suggested actions.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_auto_mode_note_e: Scheduled runs automatically enable recovered accounts and leave problem accounts for manual handling.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_auto_mode_note_d: Scheduled runs automatically enable recovered accounts and disable problem accounts.
Scheduled runs automatically enable recovered accounts, delete invalid accounts, and disable over-threshold accounts.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_history_desc: Persisted server inspection runs. Select a run to view results and logs.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_filter_no_match: No items match the current filter.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_results_desc: Shows account results for this server inspection, including suggested actions and detailed errors.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_results_desc_man: Shows the immediate inspection result produced by this manual run. Completed at {{time}}.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_results_desc_sch: Shows the inspection snapshot produced by the scheduled task at completion time. Completed at {{time}}.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_no_actions: No executable server suggestions are pending.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_execute_confirm_: {{total}} server suggestions will be executed: delete {{delete}}, disable {{disable}}, enable {{enable}}. Continue?
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_action_needs_rev: Resolve this conflict in auth file management
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_file_level_actio: Same-name auth files are merged into one file-level action
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_logs_desc: Shows persisted logs for this server inspection. Error details are kept in full.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_service_unavaila: Server Codex inspection is available only from the Manager Server-hosted panel.
monitoring.server_codex_inspection_connection_requi: Connect with a management key before viewing server inspection.
monitoring.account_quota_empty: No queryable quota is available for this account.
monitoring.realtime_table_desc: Show individual call logs in realtime with model, channel, status, latency, usage, and cost so request changes are visible immediately.
monitoring.model_pricing_desc: Maintain model pricing directly on the monitoring page and update account, model, and live-log spend instantly.
monitoring.price_storage_hint: Pricing is saved to SQLite when available; legacy browser-local pricing migrates automatically.
monitoring.workbench_desc: Buckets recent calls into approximate task groups so you can review participating models, outcomes, cost, and endpoint paths together.
monitoring.all_data_hint: {{count}} model calls in the current window.
monitoring.no_failures: No failed calls in the selected range.
monitoring.no_data: No call data is available in the current filter scope.
monitoring.no_filtered_data: No calls matched the current search filter.

logs.title: Logs Viewer
logs.error_logs_description: These are CPA-side request error logs, usually used to diagnose 4xx/5xx responses, authentication failures, rate limits, upstream errors, and similar issues. Download a file to inspect the exact cause.
logs.error_logs_request_log_enabled: Request logging is enabled, so CPA keeps this dedicated error log list empty by API contract. Disable request logging and refresh to view standalone error log files.
logs.error_logs_empty: No error request log files found
logs.error_logs_load_error: Failed to load error log list
logs.empty_desc: When "Enable logging to file" is enabled, logs will be displayed here
logs.clear_confirm: Are you sure you want to clear all logs? This action cannot be undone!
logs.show_raw_logs_hint: Show original log text for easier multi-line copy
logs.search_placeholder: Search logs by content or keyword
logs.search_empty_desc: Try a different keyword or clear the filters.
logs.double_click_copy_hint: Double-click to copy raw log line
logs.upgrade_required_desc: The current server version does not support the logs viewing feature. Please upgrade to the latest version of CLI Proxy API to use this feature.

config_management.title: Config Panel
config_management.reload_confirm_message: Reloading will discard your unsaved changes. Do you want to continue?
config_management.description: Edit config.yaml via visual editor or source file
config_management.status_disconnected: Connect to the server to load the configuration
config_management.error_yaml_not_supported: Server did not return YAML. Verify the /config.yaml endpoint is available.
config_management.visual_mode_unavailable: Visual editor unavailable until YAML syntax is fixed
config_management.visual_mode_unavailable_detail: Visual editor is unavailable because the configuration contains invalid YAML: {{message}}
config_management.visual_mode_save_blocked: Cannot save from visual mode until the YAML syntax is fixed
config_management.visual_mode_latest_yaml_invalid: The latest server configuration contains invalid YAML. Review it in source mode before saving visual changes: {{message}}

manager.title: CPA Manager Plus Configuration
manager.description: Manage CPA Manager Plus runtime settings separately from CPA configuration.
manager.boundary_hint: These settings are saved for CPAM itself. CPA usage statistics and queue retention still belong to CPA configuration.
manager.load_failed: Failed to load CPA Manager Plus configuration
manager.service_base_required: Manager Server URL could not be resolved. Open the Manager Server-hosted panel.
manager.poll_interval_retention_error: The collection polling interval must be less than or equal to the CPA usage queue retention window
manager.runtime_embedded_hint: This panel is hosted by Manager Server on the same origin. No external service URL is required.
manager.runtime_external_hint: This panel is hosted by CPA. Manager Server configuration is not available in pure CPA panel mode.
manager.external_service_hint: Manager Server URLs are no longer configured from CPA panel mode.
manager.admin_key_placeholder: Enter the Manager Server admin key
manager.admin_key_hint: Only used to access Manager Server. It is not saved into CPA configuration.
manager.admin_key_required: Manager Server admin key is required.
manager.admin_key_required_for_external: Open the Manager Server-hosted panel and use the Manager Server admin key.
manager.admin_key_required_for_rebind: Binding a different CPA requires an intentional Manager Server setup reset.
manager.cpa_management_key_placeholder: Leave empty to keep the saved key
manager.cpa_management_key_hint_embedded: After CPA resets its Management Key, enter the new key here and save. Manager Server will replace the saved key after validation succeeds.
manager.cpa_management_key_hint_external: CPA panel mode uses this key only for CPA login; Manager Server configuration is unavailable there.
manager.cpa_connection_section_hint: Edit the CPA URL and management key used by Manager Server. On save, Manager Server validates the CPA first, then stores the connection.
manager.cpa_base_url_hint: Currently saved: {{boundBase}}. Changing this URL switches the CPA bound to Manager Server.
manager.cpa_management_key_section_hint: Manager Server uses this key to access the CPA management API. After resetting the Management Key on CPA, enter the new value here; leave empty to keep the saved key. On save it is validated against CPA first before replacing the saved key.
manager.cpa_management_key_readonly_hint: The CPA URL stays bound after setup. Enter a new CPA Management Key here only after resetting it in CPA; binding a different CPA still requires resetting Manager Server setup.
manager.cpa_management_key_saving: Validating the new key against CPA…
manager.cpa_connection_risk_inline: Changing the CPA URL or management key affects proxying, usage collection, and monitoring data. Save only after confirming the target CPA is correct.
manager.cpa_connection_risk_message: You are changing the Manager Server CPA connection. Current CPA: {{currentBase}}; CPA after save: {{nextBase}}. This affects proxying, usage collection, and monitoring data. Confirm the target CPA URL and Management Key are exactly correct before continuing.
manager.binding_matched: The Manager Server is bound to the current CPA. Monitoring and usage data apply to this panel.
manager.binding_unconfigured: The Manager Server is not bound to a CPA yet. Saving will bind it to the current CPA.
manager.binding_external_disabled: CPA panel mode cannot enable Manager Server features. Open the Manager Server-hosted panel.
manager.binding_mismatched: This Manager Server is already bound to another CPA. Reset Manager Server setup before binding a different CPA.
manager.rebind_confirm_message: Binding another CPA requires resetting Manager Server setup. Current bound CPA: {{boundBase}}; requested CPA: {{currentBase}}.
manager.enable_external_confirm_message: Open the Manager Server-hosted panel to use CPAMP Manager features for {{currentBase}}.
manager.request_monitoring_hint: When enabled, CPA usage statistics are enabled and the collector starts. When disabled, the CPAM collector stops but CPA usage statistics and the CPA queue are not cleared.
manager.request_monitoring_dependency: Complete Manager Server setup and keep the bound CPA connection before enabling request monitoring.
manager.request_monitoring_queue_note: If CPA usage statistics remain enabled, re-enabling request monitoring within the CPA queue retention window may collect data retained while the CPAM collector was stopped.
manager.poll_interval_hint: Must be less than or equal to the CPA usage queue retention window. Current validation uses {{seconds}} seconds.

visual.notice: Visual mode covers common fields. Review or edit unsupported config.yaml entries in source mode.

remote.title: Remote Management
remote.description: Remote access and control panel settings
remote.allow_remote_desc: Allow management access from other hosts
remote.disable_panel_desc: Disable the built-in web control panel
remote.disable_auto_update_panel_desc: Download the panel when first missing, but never auto-update it from GitHub

auth.title: Authentication Configuration
auth.description: API keys and authentication directory settings

system.title: System Configuration
system.description: Debug, logging, statistics, and performance settings
system.commercial_mode_desc: Disable high-overhead middleware to support high concurrency
system.usage_statistics_enabled_desc: Allow CPA to publish request usage events for Manager Server to consume continuously
system.plugins_enabled_desc: Enable standard dynamic-library plugin loading; individual plugin instances are managed on the plugin page
system.plugins_dir_desc: Directory used by CPA to load plugin dynamic libraries. Leave empty to use CPA defaults.
system.plugin_store_sources_desc: One plugin store manifest URL per line. Applies to CPA's plugins.store-sources array.
system.redis_usage_queue_retention_hint: CPA defaults to 60 seconds and allows up to 3600 seconds. Leave empty to use the default. Keep Manager Server running continuously.
system.antigravity_signature_cache_desc: Prefer and validate cached thinking-block signatures
system.antigravity_signature_strict_desc: Only applies when the signature cache is disabled; validates the full Claude protobuf tree

headers.title: Header Defaults
headers.description: Default headers for Claude and Codex OAuth requests when the client omits them
headers.stabilize_device_desc: Pin OS/Arch and stabilize the software fingerprint per credential/API key
headers.identity_confuse_desc: Write codex.identity-confuse to let CPA mix Codex request identity headers for compatibility

network.title: Network Configuration
network.max_retry_credentials_hint: Leave empty to keep it unset. Set to 0 to preserve legacy behavior and try all available credentials.
network.auth_auto_refresh_workers_hint: When greater than 0, overrides the default worker count (16)
network.disable_image_generation_hint: false enables it; true disables all image generation; chat only disables non-image endpoint injection
network.force_model_prefix_desc: Unprefixed model requests only use credentials without prefix
network.passthrough_headers_desc: Forward filtered upstream response headers to downstream clients
network.disable_cooling_desc: Globally disable auth/model cooldown windows after failures

quota.title: Quota Fallback
quota.description: Fallback strategy when quota is exceeded
quota.switch_project_desc: Automatically switch to another project when quota is exceeded
quota.switch_preview_model_desc: Switch to preview model version when quota is exceeded
quota.antigravity_credits_desc: Retry once with enabledCreditTypes=["GOOGLE_ONE_AI"] when Antigravity returns quota_exhausted 429

streaming.title: Streaming Configuration
streaming.keepalive_hint: Set to 0 or leave empty to disable keepalive
streaming.bootstrap_hint: Number of retries during stream startup (before first byte)
streaming.nonstream_keepalive_hint: Send blank lines every N seconds for non-streaming responses to prevent idle timeout, set to 0 or leave empty to disable

payload.title: Payload Configuration
payload.description: Default values, raw JSON rules, override rules, and filter rules
payload.default_rules_desc: Use these default values when parameters are not specified in the request
payload.default_raw_rules_desc: When parameters are missing, write these values as raw JSON fragments such as true, 123, "high", or {"type":"object"}
payload.override_rules_desc: Force override parameter values in the request
payload.override_raw_rules_desc: Always overwrite parameter values as raw JSON fragments, useful for response_format, schemas, and other complex fields
payload.filter_rules_desc: Pre-filter upstream request body via JSON Path, automatically remove non-compliant/redundant parameters (Request Sanitization)

api_keys.label: API Keys List (api-keys)
api_keys.hint: Each entry represents an API key (consistent with 'API Key Management' page style)
api_keys.input_hint: This only modifies the local config file content, it will not sync to the API Key Management interface
api_keys.alias_hint: Aliases are saved in the CPAM database and linked to historical requests by API key hash.
api_keys.alias_delete_confirm: This only deletes the alias mapping for this hash. The API key and historical request data remain intact.
api_keys.alias_cleanup_confirm: Alias {{alias}} currently belongs to an old API key that is no longer in the config. Delete the old mapping and save it to the current API key?
api_keys.alias_cleanup_risk: Risk: historical request data for old hash {{hash}}... remains, but it may no longer show this friendly alias and may fall back to a hash label.
api_keys.alias_cleanup_cancelled: The old alias mapping was kept. Use a unique alias, or confirm deleting the old mapping before saving.
api_keys.alias_unavailable: API key aliases require the Manager Server-hosted panel.
api_keys.alias_error_too_long: Alias must be 120 characters or less
api_keys.alias_error_duplicate: Alias already exists. Use a unique alias.

payload_rules.value_raw_json: Raw JSON fragment, e.g. true, 123, "high", or {"type":"object"}

validation.port_range: Enter a valid port between 1 and 65535

usage_service_errors.connection_env_managed: Connection setup is managed by environment variables and cannot be changed from the panel.
usage_service_errors.cpa_connection_already_bound: Manager Server is already bound to one CPA. Reset Manager Server setup before binding a different CPA.
usage_service_errors.cpa_connection_required: CPA URL and Management Key are required.
usage_service_errors.cpa_connection_required_for_monitoring: CPA URL and Management Key are required when request monitoring is enabled.
usage_service_errors.management_api_validation_failed: CPA Management API validation failed. Check the CPA URL and Management Key.
usage_service_errors.management_api_config_failed: Failed to read CPA configuration from the Management API.
usage_service_errors.cpa_usage_retention_invalid: CPA usage queue retention must be greater than 0.
usage_service_errors.poll_interval_exceeds_retention: The collection polling interval must be less than or equal to the CPA usage queue retention window.
usage_service_errors.invalid_time_zone: Failed to save the time zone. Check the time zone name or make sure tzdata is installed in the runtime environment.
usage_service_errors.enable_cpa_usage_statistics_failed: Failed to enable CPA usage statistics.
usage_service_errors.setup_env_managed: Setup is managed by environment variables.
usage_service_errors.invalid_existing_management_key: The existing setup rejected this Management Key.
usage_service_errors.method_not_allowed: This operation is not supported by the current endpoint.

usage_analytics.title: Usage Analytics
usage_analytics.subtitle: Understand API usage, analyze trends and costs, and optimize resource allocation.
usage_analytics.export_coming_soon: Export will be available in a later version.
usage_analytics.view_daily_board_desc: Daily usage, cost, and health overview.
usage_analytics.view_high_cost_key_desc: Track client key concentration and abnormal spend.
usage_analytics.view_prod_credentials_desc: Watch active credentials, provider health, and quota status.
usage_analytics.view_team_weekly_desc: Weekly trend view for team usage reviews.
usage_analytics.analysis_entry_trends_desc: Inspect usage, cost, token structure, and health changes.
usage_analytics.analysis_entry_models_desc: Compare model spend, tokens, and contribution.
usage_analytics.analysis_entry_api_keys_desc: Find concentrated, failed, or abnormal client keys.
usage_analytics.analysis_entry_credentials_desc: Review auth file usage, provider health, and estimated quota.
usage_analytics.analysis_entry_heatmap_desc: Locate peak periods, cost windows, and failure-rate anomalies.
usage_analytics.token_structure_hint: Input, output, cached, and reasoning tokens stacked by time bucket.
usage_analytics.entity_trend_hint: Top entity curves are approximated from each entity's overall share — not real per-entity series.
usage_analytics.selected_credential_trend_hint: Shows only the selected credential's real time-bucket trend without share-based estimation.
usage_analytics.insights_hint: Actionable observations generated from the current analytics slice.
usage_analytics.insights_empty: No immediate risks detected in this range.
usage_analytics.insight_model_cost_high_body: The leading model owns a large share of cost. Review whether cheaper models can cover part of the workload.
usage_analytics.insight_key_long_tail_body: A small set of keys drives most traffic. Confirm ownership and expected automation volume.
usage_analytics.insight_credential_success_drop_body: One or more credentials show failed requests or lower success rate.
usage_analytics.insight_low_cost_room_body: Low-cost models already contribute traffic. Consider routing more eligible requests there.
usage_analytics.insight_provider_concentration_body: One provider carries most requests. Check fallback capacity and failover readiness.
usage_analytics.insight_cache_room_body: Cache-read share is low. Stable prompt prefixes may reduce input token cost.
usage_analytics.quota_status_hint: Estimated from recent usage where upstream quota data is unavailable.
usage_analytics.heatmap_matrix_hint: Compare API Key, auth file, or provider usage against model combinations.
usage_analytics.filter_search_placeholder: Search request ID, model, API key, credential, provider, endpoint, or failure
usage_analytics.empty_body: No matching usage records were found for the selected range and filters.
usage_analytics.trend_hint: Click a time bucket to inspect anomalies and jump to request details.
usage_analytics.chart_normalized_scale: Left axis uses {{metric}}. Other metrics are normalized by their own peak for comparison.
usage_analytics.chart_compare_scale: Comparison chart normalizes each metric by its own peak. Tooltip shows raw values.
usage_analytics.cause_traffic: Traffic or batch jobs increased in this bucket.
usage_analytics.cause_batch: A prompt, model, or client changed token consumption.
usage_analytics.cause_cache: Cache usage changed, causing more uncached input tokens.
usage_analytics.cause_request_spike: Requests increased sharply. Check batch jobs, automation, or bursty client traffic first.
usage_analytics.cause_request_drop: Requests dropped sharply. The client may have stopped calling, a queue may be empty, or upstream throttling may apply.
usage_analytics.cause_cost_spike: Cost rose significantly. Check expensive models, long contexts, or concentrated retries.
usage_analytics.cause_cost_drop: Cost dropped sharply. Requests may have decreased, a cheaper model may be in use, or outputs may be shorter.
usage_analytics.cause_token_per_request_spike: Average tokens per request increased. Inspect prompts, context stitching, and max_tokens settings.
usage_analytics.cause_cache_hit_drop: Cache hit rate dropped. Prompt structure may have changed or cache prefixes may be unstable.
usage_analytics.cause_cache_growth: Cached tokens grew. Cache reuse is higher in this bucket; compare whether cost dropped accordingly.
usage_analytics.cause_failure_rate_spike: Failure rate rose sharply. Check upstream errors, throttling, or auth configuration.
usage_analytics.cause_latency_spike: Average latency rose sharply. Model latency, network jitter, or request queueing may have increased.
usage_analytics.cause_no_clear_anomaly: This bucket is close to the previous bucket. Open request details to check individual outliers.
usage_analytics.common_views_hint: The first version reserves local view shortcuts; saved views and quick switching will be added later.
usage_analytics.overview_trend_hint: Requests, tokens, and cost are shown together for the selected time range.
usage_analytics.anomaly_points_hint: Backend-detected buckets based on request, cost, token, cache, failure-rate, and latency changes.
usage_analytics.drilldown_preview_hint: After selecting a time bucket, real request samples from that bucket are shown here.
usage_analytics.drilldown_preview_empty: Click a time bucket in the trend chart to preview request samples.
usage_analytics.provider_overview_hint: Cost, traffic share, health, cache reuse, and leading model by provider.
usage_analytics.api_key_keyword_placeholder: Search client key, account, or auth label
usage_analytics.credential_warning_summary: {{count}} anomalous credentials, {{high}} high risk, lowest success {{lowest}}.
usage_analytics.heatmap_hint: Current metric aggregated by weekday and hour to identify peak, cost, and failure-rate windows.
usage_analytics.heatmap_date_empty: No usage data for this date in the selected window.
usage_analytics.heatmap_focus_hint: Aggregated in {{timeZone}}, showing high-risk request, cost, and failure-rate windows.
usage_analytics.heatmap_failure_sample_empty: No failure-rate risks with enough samples.
usage_analytics.warning_cost: This key accounts for a high share of usage.
usage_analytics.api_key_context_empty: No execution contexts are linked to this client key.

quota_management.title: Quota Management
quota_management.description: Monitor OAuth quota status for Antigravity, Codex, Claude, Kimi, and xAI credentials.
quota_management.card_idle_hint: Use the top "Refresh all credentials" button to fetch the latest quota data.
quota_management.search_placeholder: Search name, type, account ID, plan, or error status
quota_management.search_empty_desc: Adjust the search keyword or clear the search filter.

codex_reauth.title: Codex Re-login
codex_reauth.callback_placeholder: Paste the callback URL after OAuth login completes
codex_reauth.waiting: After OAuth login completes, paste the callback URL and submit it.
codex_reauth.missing_state: The OAuth login link is missing its state value. Refresh the link and try again.
codex_reauth.same_account_hint: Use the target account to complete OAuth login. CPA saves Codex credentials by the logged-in account; the same account usually overwrites the matching credential.
codex_reauth.rerun_hint: Re-login is complete. Run the inspection again to refresh the account status.

plugin_management.title: Plugin Management
plugin_management.description: Review discovered and registered plugins, then manage instance toggles, config fields, and resource links.
plugin_management.config_not_found: Unable to read plugin config because the backend could not find this plugin.
plugin_management.unsupported_backend: The current backend does not expose the plugin management API. Use a newer backend build that includes plugin management endpoints, then restart the service.
plugin_management.global_disabled_hint: plugins.enabled is false, so enabled plugin instances will not become effective.
plugin_management.search_placeholder: Search plugin ID, name, author, or resource...
plugin_management.no_plugins_desc: No plugins were found in the plugin directory or runtime registry.
plugin_management.reinstall_confirm_message: This will delete {{name}} first, then reinstall it from the plugin store. If deletion succeeds but installation fails, the plugin will remain deleted. Continue?
plugin_management.reinstall_store_entry_not_found: This plugin was not found in the plugin store, so it cannot be reinstalled.
plugin_management.reinstall_source_required: This plugin exists in multiple store sources, so the reinstall source cannot be determined automatically.
plugin_management.reinstall_delete_restart_required: The plugin is loaded and cannot be deleted online. Restart CPA, then reinstall it.
plugin_management.reinstall_restart_required: Restart the service to load the reinstalled plugin version
plugin_management.delete_confirm_message: This will delete the local file for {{name}} if it exists and remove its saved config. This action cannot be undone.
plugin_management.delete_restart_required: The plugin is loaded and cannot be deleted online. Restart CPA, then try deleting it again.
plugin_management.enabled_hint: Controls the enabled field for this plugin instance under plugins.configs.
plugin_management.no_config_fields: This plugin does not declare visual config fields.
plugin_management.validation_failed: Fix plugin config form errors first
plugin_management.invalid_enum: Choose one of the declared enum values

plugin_store.title: Plugin Store
plugin_store.description: Browse the plugin registry, then install or update plugins for the current backend.
plugin_store.load_failed: Failed to load the plugin store
plugin_store.loading_desc: Reading plugin sources and installation status
plugin_store.unsupported_backend: The current backend does not expose the plugin store API. Use a newer backend build that includes plugin store endpoints, then restart the service.
plugin_store.registry_failed: Failed to reach the plugin registry
plugin_store.global_disabled_hint: plugins.enabled is false, so installed plugins will not become effective.
plugin_store.source_required: This plugin exists in multiple store sources. Use a source-specific plugin card and try again.
plugin_store.search_placeholder: Search plugin ID, name, author, or tag...
plugin_store.install_confirm_message: Download {{target}} from the plugin registry and install it into the local plugin directory?
plugin_store.install_security_warning_title: Third-party plugins run with full backend privileges
plugin_store.install_security_warning_message: Plugins execute code inside the proxy service and can read your credentials and traffic. Install only plugins you trust, and be especially cautious with anything not published by the official router-for-me organization.
plugin_store.update_confirm_message: Download and install the latest version of {{target}}?
plugin_store.reinstall_confirm_message: This will delete {{target}} first, then reinstall it from the plugin store. If deletion succeeds but installation fails, the plugin will remain deleted. Continue?
plugin_store.delete_confirm_message: This will delete the local file for {{name}} if it exists and remove its saved config. This action cannot be undone.
plugin_store.delete_restart_required: The plugin is loaded and cannot be deleted online. Restart CPA, then try deleting it again.
plugin_store.reinstall_delete_restart_required: The plugin is loaded and cannot be deleted online. Restart CPA, then reinstall it.
plugin_store.restart_required_notice: Restart the service to load the new plugin version
plugin_store.restart_required_banner: Restart the service to apply the new version of: {{plugins}}
plugin_store.no_plugins_desc: The plugin registry did not return any plugins.
plugin_store.no_matches_desc: No plugins match the current search or filter.
plugin_store.gate_warning: Third-party plugins run with full backend access.
plugin_store.gate_effect_runs_code: This plugin will run third-party code inside the proxy backend and may access credentials, requests, and responses.
plugin_store.gate_effect_no_review: It is not published or reviewed by the official router-for-me organization. Future versions may change behavior.
plugin_store.gate_effect_restart: Installing it changes the local plugin directory and may require a service restart.
plugin_store.gate_untrusted_alert: Continue only if you fully trust this plugin's author, repository, and source.
plugin_store.gate_step1_action: I want to install this plugin
plugin_store.gate_step2_action: I have read and understand these risks
plugin_store.gate_step3_hint: Enter the exact identifier shown above.

plugin_resource.unsupported_backend: The current backend does not expose the plugin page API. Use a newer backend build that includes plugin management endpoints, then restart the service.
plugin_resource.not_found_desc: This plugin page is not declared by the current backend plugin list.
plugin_resource.empty_src_desc: This plugin menu does not provide an embeddable page URL.

notification.api_key_invalid_chars: API key can only contain letters, numbers, and symbols
notification.gemini_multi_input_required: Please enter at least one Gemini key
notification.gemini_multi_summary: Gemini bulk add finished: {{success}} added, {{skipped}} skipped, {{failed}} failed
notification.codex_base_url_required: Please enter the Codex Base URL
notification.openai_provider_required: Please fill in provider name and Base URL
notification.openai_test_url_required: Please provide a valid Base URL before testing
notification.openai_test_key_required: Please add at least one API key before testing
notification.openai_test_model_required: Please select a model to test
notification.commercial_mode_restart_required: Commercial mode setting changed. Please restart the service for it to take effect

accountPolicy.section_hint: Only affects new request-monitoring events. Queued or running tasks continue to finish.
accountPolicy.runtime_note: Unlocked switches are saved to this Manager Server and take effect without restarting. Environment variables still lock their matching switches.
accountPolicy.group_quota_description: Controls how auth files are cooled down and recovered after request monitoring detects quota exhaustion.
accountPolicy.group_auth_issues_description: Controls how revoked tokens, unavailable auth, and deactivated workspaces enter the pending handling flow.
accountPolicy.codexQuotaCooldown_summary: Temporarily disables a Codex auth file after confirmed short-window usage exhaustion, then recovers it after reset.
accountPolicy.codexQuotaCooldown_description: When request monitoring confirms a Codex short-window usage limit, it temporarily disables the auth file and auto-recovers after the reset time.
codexQuotaCooldown_behavior: Does not recover manually disabled auth files
accountPolicy.authIssueQueue_summary: Records high-confidence auth issues as candidates for manual handling on the Auth Issues page.
accountPolicy.authIssueQueue_description: When request monitoring detects a revoked token, unavailable auth, or a deactivated workspace, it records a candidate here for manual handling.
authIssueQueue_behavior: Handled manually on the Auth Issues page
Does not record new candidates when disabled
Existing pending candidates can still be processed
accountPolicy.authIssueAutoDisable_summary: Automatically disables high-confidence delete / reauth candidates first, while keeping the manual review flow.
accountPolicy.authIssueAutoDisable_description: When enabled, high-confidence candidates auto-disable the auth file first and still require your manual handling.
authIssueAutoDisable_behavior: Auto-disables only high-confidence delete / reauth candidates
Re-reads auth files and verifies identity before disabling
accountPolicy.authIssueAutoDisable_dependency_note: Enable the Auth Issue Pending Queue first.
accountPolicy.authIssueAutoDisable_configured_dependen: Enabled, pending the Auth Issue Pending Queue.
accountPolicy.authIssueAutoDisable_confirm_body: When enabled, Manager Server disables matching auth files after request monitoring detects high-confidence auth issues.
accountPolicy.authIssueAutoDisable_confirm_disable_onl: It only disables matching auth files and never deletes them.
accountPolicy.authIssueAutoDisable_confirm_no_recovery: It never auto-recovers them; you still handle the candidate on the Auth Issues page.
accountPolicy.authIssueAutoDisable_confirm_requires_qu: This capability depends on the Auth Issue Pending Queue being effective.
accountPolicy.authIssueAutoDisable_confirm: Enable auth issue auto-disable? It only disables matching auth files, never deletes them, never auto-recovers them, and still requires manual handling.
accountPolicy.env_locked_hint: This switch is locked by an environment variable. Update the service environment variable and restart to change it.
accountPolicy.env_locked_reason: Locked by the {{envKey}} environment variable. Update it and restart the service to change this switch.
accountPolicy.gating_note: Disabling a switch only stops new request-monitoring events from being processed. Tasks already queued or in progress will continue to finish.
